title: Smoky Crispy Russian Beef Pierogi
description: A weeknight twist on traditional Russian pierogi, featuring smoky paprika and a crispy exterior.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, warm water, salt, and sugar. Mix until the dough comes together, then knead for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 2 equal pieces and roll each piece into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat and cook the chopped onion and garlic until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped beef to the skillet and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Stir in the smoked paprika and cook for an additional minute.
Spoon the beef mixture onto the dough circles, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the dough over the filling and press the edges together to seal the pierogi.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pierogi in batches for 3-5 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
In a large skillet, heat a couple of tablespoons of oil over medium heat and cook the pierogi until crispy and golden brown on both sides.
Serve the pierogi hot with your favorite toppings, such as sour cream, fried onions, or chopped fresh dill.
